verdict: The single best mandi-price source for a Vercel-hosted demo TODAY is the new Agmarknet 2.0 open API (api.agmarknet.gov.in/v1) — verified live this session with no key, no signup, returning today's (07-07-2026) modal prices for Tomato (452 records, 18 states) and Wheat (207 records) in clean JSON. The famous data.gov.in resource 9ef84268-d588-465a-a308-a864a43d0070 could NOT be verified: api.data.gov.in timed out from the local network AND was ECONNREFUSED from a second fetcher (NIC infra appears to block non-Indian/cloud IPs today), so do not bet the live demo on it; same story for e-NAM (ECONNREFUSED). For agronomy grounding, there is no machine-readable ICAR 'package of practices' API — the right move is an embedded, citation-backed knowledge table (provided in embed_data, grounded in ICAR/SAU package-of-practices norms and the FAO Irrigation Water Management manual Table 14, which I fetched and verified). Caveat learned from live data: Karnataka mandis barely report to Agmarknet (0 tomato rows; they use the state ReMS platform), so pick UP/Maharashtra/TN/MP districts for the scripted demo moment.
demo_plan: DEMO (all verified working right now, zero signup): (1) Mandi prices — create a Next.js route handler that POSTs to https://api.agmarknet.gov.in/v1/daily-price-arrival/report with JSON body {"group":"6","commodity":"65","state":"100000","district":"100001","market":"100002","variety":"100007","grade":"100003","type":"100004","from_date":"<yesterday YYYY-MM-DD>","to_date":"<today YYYY-MM-DD>","page":"1","limit":"500"} (all values as strings; Content-Type: application/json; no auth/Origin needed). Response: data.records[0].data = array of rows with fields state_name, district_name, market_name, cmdt_grp_name, cmdt_name, variety_name, grade_name, min_price, max_price, model_price (strings with commas, e.g. "1,200.00"), unit_name_price ("Rs./Quintal"), arrival_qty, unit_name_arrival ("Metric Tonnes"), arrival_date ("DD-MM-YYYY"); data.records[0].pagination[0] = {total_count,total_pages,current_page,items_per_page}. IMPORTANT quirks found by testing: the "group" field is REQUIRED for the commodity filter to apply; passing a specific state id did NOT filter results (server ignores it) — fetch all-India with limit=500 (works, returned all 452 rows in one call) and filter by state_name/district_name in your code; strip commas before parseFloat. Use the magic 'All' ids: state 100000, district 100001, market 100002, variety 100007, grade 100003, type 100004 (=Price; 100005=Arrival, 100006=Both). Commodity+group ids for your crops are in embed_data. Cache the response server-side 30-60 min (calls take 2-6s). Optionally call GET https://api.agmarknet.gov.in/v1/daily-price-arrival/filters once (verified: 604 commodities, 37 states, 740 districts, 4182 markets, 2133 varieties) — but better to hardcode the id map from embed_data. (2) Crop recommendation engine — ship the embed_data agronomy table in code and let Gemini reason over it (soil type + pH + season + water availability from the farmer → score crops); cite 'ICAR/State Agricultural University Package of Practices; FAO Irrigation Water Management Manual (Table 14)' in the UI footnote. Do NOT attempt live calls to api.data.gov.in or enam.gov.in in the demo — both were unreachable from non-India networks during this session and would fail on stage from Vercel's US regions.
production_plan: PRODUCTION: (1) Primary prices: keep Agmarknet 2.0 API (it is the official DMI/Ministry of Agriculture source; the data.gov.in resource is just a re-publication of the same Agmarknet data) with a nightly ETL into your own Postgres so the app never depends on govt uptime; add the data.gov.in API (resource 9ef84268-d588-465a-a308-a864a43d0070, params: api-key=<your key from data.gov.in registration>&format=json&limit=&offset=&filters[state.keyword]=&filters[commodity]=; record fields historically: state, district, market, commodity, variety, grade, arrival_date, min_price, max_price, modal_price) as a redundant feed — but run those fetchers from an India region (Vercel functions pinned to bom1, or a small Mumbai VM/cron) because NIC blocks many foreign/cloud IPs, which is exactly what we hit today. Register for a free personal API key on data.gov.in (instant, but a signup — hence not for today). (2) e-NAM (enam.gov.in) — 1000+ integrated mandis, has dashboard trade data but no public API and is India-IP-gated; pursue via the e-NAM/SFAC partnership route; use as a deck citation ('integrates with e-NAM') not a dependency. (3) Agronomy: license/ingest state SAU Packages of Practices (TNAU Agritech Portal, PAU, UAS Bangalore/Raichur PoP PDFs) plus ICAR-KVK advisories into a curated, versioned knowledge base reviewed by an agronomist; ground Gemini responses on it via RAG. (4) MSP: the Agmarknet v1 API also exposes MSP report endpoints (/alltypeofreportMSP, /abovemspmarketprice, /belowMSPstatewise — seen in the app bundle, not yet exercised) worth wiring in production for 'price vs MSP' alerts.
embed_data: {"source":"ICAR/SAU Package of Practices norms; FAO Irrigation Water Management Training Manual Table 14 (water_need ranges verified from fao.org/4/s2022e/s2022e07.htm); yields = typical well-managed farmer yields (national averages are lower)","units":{"water_need_mm":"[min,max] mm per season, field-level","typical_yield_q_per_acre":"quintals/acre (1 t/ha = 4.05 q/acre)"},"crops":[{"crop":"Rice (Paddy)","seasons":["kharif","rabi (S. India)"],"soil_types":["clay","clay loam","alluvial"],"ph_range":[5.5,7.5],"water_need_mm":[1100,1250],"duration_days":[110,150],"typical_yield_q_per_acre":22,"notes":"Puddled transplanted; crop ET is 450-700mm (FAO) but field duty with puddling/percolation is ~1100-1250mm. Needs standing water at tillering."},{"crop":"Wheat","seasons":["rabi"],"soil_types":["loam","clay loam","alluvial"],"ph_range":[6.0,7.5],"water_need_mm":[450,650],"duration_days":[120,150],"typical_yield_q_per_acre":18,"notes":"4-6 irrigations; CRI stage (21 DAS) irrigation is critical. Sow by mid-Nov; terminal heat cuts yield."},{"crop":"Maize","seasons":["kharif","rabi","zaid"],"soil_types":["sandy loam","loam","clay loam (drained)"],"ph_range":[5.5,7.5],"water_need_mm":[500,800],"duration_days":[90,120],"typical_yield_q_per_acre":20,"notes":"Very sensitive to waterlogging; tasseling-silking is the critical water stage. Rabi/spring maize yields highest."},{"crop":"Cotton","seasons":["kharif"],"soil_types":["black cotton (regur)","deep alluvial"],"ph_range":[6.0,8.0],"water_need_mm":[700,1300],"duration_days":[150,180],"typical_yield_q_per_acre":8,"notes":"Yield = seed cotton (kapas). Long season; pink bollworm scouting essential; avoid waterlogged fields."},{"crop":"Soybean","seasons":["kharif"],"soil_types":["black (vertisol)","clay loam","loam"],"ph_range":[6.0,7.5],"water_need_mm":[450,700],"duration_days":[90,110],"typical_yield_q_per_acre":7,"notes":"MP/Maharashtra belt; mostly rainfed (needs 600-1000mm rainfall); seed inoculation with Rhizobium recommended."},{"crop":"Tomato","seasons":["kharif","rabi","zaid (irrigated)"],"soil_types":["sandy loam","loam, well-drained, organic-rich"],"ph_range":[6.0,7.0],"water_need_mm":[400,800],"duration_days":[110,140],"typical_yield_q_per_acre":130,"notes":"Transplanted at 25-30 days; staking + drip raises yield sharply; very price-volatile crop."},{"crop":"Chilli","seasons":["kharif","rabi (irrigated)"],"soil_types":["loam","black","sandy loam, well-drained"],"ph_range":[6.0,7.0],"water_need_mm":[600,900],"duration_days":[150,180],"typical_yield_q_per_acre":8,"notes":"Yield = dry red chilli (green chilli ~4-5x more). Thrips/mites drive leaf curl; Guntur/Khammam belt."},{"crop":"Groundnut","seasons":["kharif","rabi/summer (S. India)"],"soil_types":["sandy loam","light red","calcareous"],"ph_range":[6.0,7.5],"water_need_mm":[500,700],"duration_days":[100,130],"typical_yield_q_per_acre":9,"notes":"Yield = unshelled pods. Needs loose soil for pegging; gypsum at flowering; pegging-to-pod-fill is critical for water."},{"crop":"Mustard","seasons":["rabi"],"soil_types":["loam","sandy loam","alluvial"],"ph_range":[6.0,7.5],"water_need_mm":[240,400],"duration_days":[110,140],"typical_yield_q_per_acre":6,"notes":"Low water need, 1-2 irrigations (flowering + siliqua fill); tolerates mild salinity/alkalinity; Rajasthan-UP-Haryana belt."},{"crop":"Gram (Chickpea)","seasons":["rabi"],"soil_types":["loam","clay loam","black (residual moisture)"],"ph_range":[6.0,7.5],"water_need_mm":[250,400],"duration_days":[100,120],"typical_yield_q_per_acre":6,"notes":"Often grown on conserved soil moisture; excess irrigation/N causes vegetative overgrowth; wilt-resistant varieties in continuous-gram fields."},{"crop":"Onion","seasons":["kharif","late kharif","rabi"],"soil_types":["loam","alluvial","medium black, friable"],"ph_range":[6.0,7.0],"water_need_mm":[350,550],"duration_days":[100,140],"typical_yield_q_per_acre":120,"notes":"Rabi crop stores best; stop irrigation 10-15 days before harvest; Nashik/Maharashtra dominates supply."},{"crop":"Potato","seasons":["rabi (plains)","summer (hills)"],"soil_types":["sandy loam","loam, well-drained, K-rich"],"ph_range":[5.2,6.5],"water_need_mm":[500,700],"duration_days":[90,120],"typical_yield_q_per_acre":100,"notes":"Prefers slightly acidic soil (scab risk above pH 7); earthing-up at 30 DAS; late blight in cool-humid spells; UP/WB/Bihar belt."},{"crop":"Sugarcane","seasons":["annual (spring/adsali planting)"],"soil_types":["deep loam","clay loam","medium black"],"ph_range":[6.5,7.5],"water_need_mm":[1500,2500],"duration_days":[300,365],"typical_yield_q_per_acre":350,"notes":"Highest water user (FAO 1500-2500mm); formative phase (0-120d) irrigation critical; ratoon crops cut cost ~30%."},{"crop":"Bajra (Pearl Millet)","seasons":["kharif","zaid (summer)"],"soil_types":["sandy","sandy loam, light"],"ph_range":[6.5,7.8],"water_need_mm":[350,500],"duration_days":[75,90],"typical_yield_q_per_acre":7,"notes":"Most drought-hardy cereal; viable at 250-400mm rainfall; tolerates salinity better than most crops; Rajasthan/Gujarat belt."}],"agmarknet":{"api_base":"https://api.agmarknet.gov.in/v1","report_endpoint":"POST /daily-price-arrival/report","all_ids":{"state":"100000","district":"100001","market":"100002","variety":"100007","grade":"100003","type_price":"100004"},"commodity_ids":{"Wheat":{"commodity":"1","group":"1"},"Paddy(Common)":{"commodity":"2","group":"1"},"Rice":{"commodity":"3","group":"1"},"Maize":{"commodity":"4","group":"1"},"Bajra(Pearl Millet/Cumbu)":{"commodity":"28","group":"1"},"Bengal Gram(Gram)(Whole)":{"commodity":"6","group":"2"},"Groundnut":{"commodity":"10","group":"3"},"Mustard":{"commodity":"12","group":"3"},"Soyabean":{"commodity":"13","group":"3"},"Cotton":{"commodity":"15","group":"4"},"Onion":{"commodity":"23","group":"6"},"Potato":{"commodity":"24","group":"6"},"Tomato":{"commodity":"65","group":"6"},"Green Chilli":{"commodity":"73","group":"6"},"Dry Chillies":{"commodity":"113","group":"7"},"Sugarcane":{"commodity":"122","group":"10"}},"key_state_ids":{"Andhra Pradesh":2,"Bihar":5,"Gujarat":11,"Haryana":12,"Karnataka":16,"Madhya Pradesh":19,"Maharashtra":20,"Odisha":26,"Punjab":28,"Rajasthan":29,"Tamil Nadu":31,"Telangana":32,"Uttar Pradesh":34,"West Bengal":36},"caveats":["group param required or commodity filter is ignored","state filter not honored server-side in testing - filter client-side on state_name","prices are strings with commas e.g. 1,200.00","arrival_date format DD-MM-YYYY","Karnataka mandis largely absent (state uses ReMS)"]}}

findings.source_name: Agmarknet 2.0 open API — daily price/arrival report (Directorate of Marketing & Inspection, GoI)
findings.verification_note: Fetched live during session: Tomato (commodity 65, group 6) returned 452 records dated 06/07-07-2026 across 18 states in one limit=500 call; Wheat (commodity 1, group 1) returned 207 records for one day. Row fields: state_name, district_name, market_name, cmdt_grp_name, cmdt_name, variety_name, grade_name, min_price, max_price, model_price ('1,000.00'), unit_name_price ('Rs./Quintal'), arrival_qty, unit_name_arrival ('Metric Tonnes'), arrival_date ('07-07-2026'). Pagination at data.records[0].pagination[0] = {total_count:452,total_pages,current_page,items_per_page}. No API key, no Origin/Referer needed. Quirks verified: omit 'group' or use wrong param names and the commodity filter is silently ignored (returns everything); specific state ids (tried state/state_id=16 Karnataka) are NOT honored — filter client-side; arrays rejected ('Invalid input: int() argument...'). Date range limited to 1 year per filters metadata.
findings.notes: THE demo endpoint. Official govt source (agmarknet.gov.in is the Ministry of Agriculture/DMI portal, rebuilt 2025-26 as a React SPA on GCP, globally reachable). Endpoint discovered from the production JS bundle main.a96c4fad.js. Response takes 2-6s — cache server-side.

findings.source_name: Agmarknet 2.0 open API — filters (commodity/state/district/market/variety/grade master data)
findings.verification_note: Fetched live: 524KB JSON, {status:true, data:{cmdt_data:[604 commodities {cmdt_id,cmdt_name,cmdt_group_id}], state_data:[37 {state_id,state_name}], district_data:[740], market_data:[4182], variety_data:[2133], grade_data:[18], cmdt_group_data:[16], type_data:[Price=100004,Arrival=100005,Both=100006], range_data (allowed date window)]}. Key ids extracted: Wheat=1, Paddy(Common)=2, Rice=3, Maize=4, Gram=6, Groundnut=10, Mustard=12, Soyabean=13, Cotton=15, Onion=23, Potato=24, Bajra=28, Tomato=65, Green Chilli=73, Dry Chillies=113 (group 7), Sugarcane=122 (group 10).
findings.notes: Call once at build time or hardcode the id map (provided in embed_data). 'All' sentinel ids: state 100000, district 100001, market 100002, variety 100007, grade 100003.

findings.source_name: FAO Irrigation Water Management Training Manual no.3 — Table 14 crop water needs + Table 6 growing periods
findings.verification_note: Fetched live; extracted exact values: Rice 450-700mm, Wheat 450-650, Maize 500-800, Cotton 700-1300, Soybean 450-700, Groundnut 500-700, Tomato 400-800, Pepper(chilli) 600-900, Bean 300-500 mm/total growing period; growing periods: Rice 90-150d, Wheat 120-150, Maize 125-180, Cotton 180-195, Soybean 135-150, Groundnut 130-140, Tomato 135-180, Pepper 120-210.
findings.notes: Authoritative grounding for the water_need_mm column in embed_data. Note for rice: FAO figure is crop ET; Indian puddled-paddy field duty is ~1100-1250mm (reflected in embed table with an explanatory note).

findings.source_name: TNAU Agritech Portal — Crop Production / Package of Practices (Tamil Nadu Agricultural University)
findings.verification_note: Fetched live (HTTP 200 from 14.139.187.9): confirmed directory of crop production guides for cereals/pulses/oilseeds/millets with sections on Seasons & Varieties, Irrigation Mgmt, Nutrient Mgmt; sub-page URLs extracted. HTML pages only — no API, no structured data.
findings.notes: Best-known SAU package-of-practices source; cite it (plus ICAR) as the authority behind the embedded agronomy table. Do not scrape live in the demo.

findings.verification_note: Fetched live (HTTP 200 from 144.16.144.82): confirmed ICAR homepage with Crop Science division links. No machine-readable package-of-practices API exists anywhere on ICAR properties.
findings.notes: Cite 'grounded in ICAR & State Agricultural University Packages of Practices' in UI/deck. Production path: curate PoP PDFs (ICAR, TNAU, PAU, UAS) into a reviewed RAG knowledge base.

findings.verification_note: Fetched live: returns the Agmarknet 2.0 React SPA shell (1KB, 'You need to enable JavaScript') — the old scrape-able .aspx price tables that most tutorials/StackOverflow answers reference are GONE.
findings.notes: Important negative result: any code/blog pattern based on SearchCmmMkt.aspx scraping is dead. Use the v1 JSON API above.
